entry.title: Re: Can you share an xsuaa service accross different spac...
entry.content:
Authorization & Trust management service - shared tenant-mode

The tenant-mode shared allows you to share a UAA service instance of type application in a multitenant scenario. This is a central requirement of a multitenant application, as otherwise a separate UAA service instance would be required in each and every Consumer Subaccount. As all Tenants share the same XSUAA instance and only the authentication is delegated to the respective Tenant identity zone, this is a great process simplification.

entry.title: Why Generative UI is Redefining the Future of Enterprise Software
entry.content:
The world of user interfaces is evolving rapidly, spurred by the rise of consumer AI and shifting expectations for business software. Employees want the same intuitive and conversational experiences they enjoy at home to be reflected in their workplace tools. While text-based interactions are flexible and user-friendly, they struggle with structured data. Graphical user interfaces (GUIs) excel in presenting structured information, but they are rigid and costly to customize.

Enter generative UI: the game-changing solution bridging text and GUI. Generative UI allows software interfaces to dynamically adapt to each user’s intent, context, and task. By leveraging SAP Joule and Joule Agents, these interfaces materialize on demand, grounded in real-time, accurate data, while enabling seamless execution of complex workflows.

Generative UI is the future of enterprise software. It’s not just about dashboards—it’s about interfaces that empower users to make decisions and drive action with precision and confidence.

Read the full blog to learn how generative UI is redefining business applications.

entry.title: Re: Adding HANA DB in cloud ALM
entry.content:
3451241 - Add SAP HANA Cloud system with XS advanced and streaming analytics to SAP Cloud ALM.

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database

 

Prerequisites

For Configuration and Security Analysis

  • You have a subscription for SAP HANA Cloud.

  • The information for your system or service has been imported to the Landscape Management app in SAP Cloud ALM. This happens daily with an automatic landscape import job. After subscribing to SAP Cloud ALM, you need to wait up to 24 hours for the job to run, for the first time.

For Health Monitoring:

  • You have access to the SAP BTP Cockpit.

  • Your user in SAP BTP Cockpit needs to be a member with the space role Space Developer for the space where you want to create the SAP HANA Cloud database instance.

Setup in SAP HANA Cloud

To set up Health Monitoring for SAP HANA Cloud, you need to create a service key. This isn't required to set up Configuration and Security Analysis.

To enable OAuth authentication between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP HANA Cloud, create a service key for the SAP HANA Cloud instance. If there's already an existing service key, you can use this service key for the connection with SAP Cloud ALM.

  1. In the SAP BTP Cockpit, access the sub-account used for SAP HANA Cloud.

  2. Choose Instances and Subscriptions.

  3. Find the instance with the service SAP HANA Cloud and choose hana for the database instance that you want to connect. Select the row of the instance.

  4. Go to the Service Keys tab.

  5. Choose Create.

  6. Enter a name for the service key.

  7. Choose Create.

Setup in SAP Cloud ALM

For Configuration and Security Analysis, there's no additional setup needed in SAP HANA Cloud.

The integration between SAP Cloud ALM and many SAP cloud services is orchestrated by SAP-Managed Connectivity.

SAP-managed connectivity uses SAP's infrastructure for OpenTelemetry-based data collection to report monitoring data to SAP Cloud ALM.

To activate the data collection, activate the monitoring in the respective SAP Cloud ALM monitoring app. By activating the data collection, you also give consent for the data exchange.

For details, check the documentation for the supported use cases:

To set up Health Monitoring, you need to create an endpoint in SAP Cloud ALM.

For Health Monitoring: Create an HTTP Endpoint

Before starting the setup, ensure that the information for your SAP HANA Cloud database has been imported from the System Landscape Information Service (SLIS) into the Landscape Management of SAP Cloud ALM.

  1. Open the Landscape Management app from the launchpad and select your service under Services & Systems.

  2. Choose the service name or choose  (Navigation) at the end of the row.

  3. On the Endpoints tab, choose Add to create a new endpoint.

  4. Enter a description.

  5. Choose the use-case you want to use in SAP Cloud ALM, for example Health Monitoring.

  6. Overwrite the value in the field Root URL as follows:

    Find the parameter host in the service key.

    Replace the part <hostname>.hana with api.gateway.orchestration.

    Example: Host 1ab123a4-1234-12b3-a123-1d234e567890.hana.trial-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com is changed to Root URL https://api.gateway.orchestration.trial-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com.

  7. Choose OAuth2ClientCredentials. Enter the following values from the SAP HANA Cloud service key. You can also copy the entire service key to the clipboard and use the Paste Service Key function.

    1. Client ID: clientid

    2. Client Secret: clientsecret

    3. Token Service URL: tokenurl extended by /oauth/token

  8. Save your endpoint.

Activate the Service in the Monitoring Apps

For Configuration and Security Analysis:

 

For Health Monitoring:

  • After creating the endpoint in SAP Cloud ALM, go to the Health Monitoring app and include SAP HANA Cloud in the scope.

3635636 - Error creating Endpoint for SAP HANA Cloud Database in Cloud ALM Landscape Management

entry.title: Re: Can't edit Open Deals in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Based your description, I understand you have issue with editing open deals in Deal Execution card in SAP for Me.

As you can see in the attachment, the required authorization to edit this card is "Partner - End Customer Sales & Adoption ".

Unfortunately, we here at SAP Support do not have relevant authorization to support SAP for Me Partner Dashboards or any of the related partner cards.

The authorizations required for Partner Dashboards are different than Customer Dashboards.

In order to get the full benefits of SAP for Me, ensure that you contact your Partner User Administrator (previously called Partner Security Manager) to provide you with the permissions you need for your specific role.

If you do not know who is your Partner User Administrator, just check SAP for Me > My Important Contacts 

To get further help, you may refer to the attachment to use get support channel to contact the responsible partner support team.

Please also see this KBA for more info:

3165784 - How to get assistance for Partner Dashboards and the related partner cards - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Why you should migrate FSVs to the Manage Global Hierarchies app
entry.content:
Features are missing: 

1-foreign currency reval requires an FSV.  This seems to be required to be maintained in OB58 (e.g. Manage Global Hierarchies do not show up in "old" foreign curr valuation".

2-EBRR requires FSV's to be created in OB58 (sap note:  Migrate FSVs to Global Hierarchies | SAP Help Portal)

As far as I know there is no copy Global Hierarchy to OB58.  Until these two apps above are resolved to read global hierarchies, there is a missing feature set that prevents completely moving away from OB58.

entry.title: Re: Joule in Ariba Guided Buying: “Open in App” fails – sap.ushell.Container undefined, Fiori 3 CSS
entry.content: Hi Kishore, This issue typically occurs when the role assignments between Ariba and BWZ are not aligned. Navigational prompts are tied to Ariba roles, and without the correct role, the navigational flow will not execute successfully. To resolve this, please ensure the user has the required role assigned in Ariba and then sync the user back to BWZ. Verify the user roles in BWZ. Before retesting, clear your cookies and log in again.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Performance Form Business Rule Returning -1972 for Unofficial Rating in M2
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am configuring a business rule for a Performance Management form (Role Readiness Assessment) and I am facing an issue where the rule always retrieves –1972 for the Unofficial Rating (Employee Rating) value.

As a result, the GAP calculation and my comment‑requirement logic never execute.

🔍Scenario

I have a competency section (Role Specific Competencies) where:

  • Employee enters Unofficial Rating in M1
  • Manager enters Official Rating in M2
  • In M2, if the GAP > 2, I want to make section comments mandatory


🔧My Rule Logic (simplified)

  • Check current step = Manager Review
  • Check Unofficial Rating is not null and not –1972
  • Compare: Official Rating – Unofficial Rating > 2
  • If Yes → Set Section Comment Required = YES

However, the rule always treats the Unofficial Rating as –1972, even when the manager is editing in M2.

The XML shows that the form is in Step M2. If the "Unofficial Rating" (Employee Rating) was hidden or not provided in the previous step (M1), the system will return -1972 when the rule attempts to pull that value for a gap calculation.

 Any recommended patterns to ensure GAP logic works consistently?

Thank you in advance — I appreciate any guidance from the community!

entry.title: Introducing SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine
entry.content:
Recently SAP announced the availability of SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine.  This is a very important expansion of the SAP Solution Extension Portfolio from BlackLine which all SAP by BlackLine customers can begin to utilize right away.  This article introduces and summarizes the key features and benefits of the new SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine solution. 

First, introducing SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine provides enhanced capabilities for the SAP + BlackLine gold standard for record to report as noted below.   Also with this announcement, BlackLine is rebranding its solution extensions by BlackLine portfolio from "SAP financial close solutions by BlackLine" to "SAP accounting automation solutions by BlackLine” by using a name that better reflects the end-to-end automation capabilities that the solutions provide.

 

Second, SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine is designed to bring comprehensive line of sight to the enterprise financial close by improving visibility and automation through unified accounting processes.  SAP Accounting Automation by Blackline leverages specific capabilities of BlackLine Studio360 to make this happen.  So for the first time, joint SAP and solution extensions by BlackLine customers can now integrate the available BlackLine process templates and the status of BlackLine sub-processes right from within SAP Advanced Financial Closing during the financial close by adding a BlackLine Process template to a SAP Advanced Financial Closing task as shown below.  This can further improve efficiency and create capacity across the financial close.

 

The first version of SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine is focused on orchestration of BlackLine sub-processes and integration with SAP Advanced Financial Closing and has successfully completed SAP’s Premium Qualification process with an official launch date of Feb 16, 2026.   An example of a BlackLine process template showing BlackLine sub-processes which can now be included in a closing task for SAP Advanced Financial Closing is shown below.  End users can also create and modify additional process templates establishing a library of process templates helpful for continuous improvement activities related to the financial close each month. 

 

An example BlackLine process template showing BlackLine sub-processes which can now be included in a closing task for SAP Advanced Financial Closing.

 

 

 

The new integration component within SAP Advanced Financial Closing enables the connection to SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine and is available by default for SAP Advanced Financial Closing customers on version 6.0 available Feb 16th 2026.

SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine is currently a no-charge entitlement to existing and new solution extensions by BlackLine (SolEx) customers.  The current product scope of "SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine“ is orchestration of BlackLine sub-processes and integration with SAP Advanced Financial Closing.

Existing solution extensions by BlackLine (SolEx) customers should submit a customer support ticket to SAP to request activation of "SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine“.   Future capabilities of BlackLine Studio360 will be evaluated for inclusion in SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine.   

So in conclusion, SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine is a very important expansion to solution extensions by BlackLine portfolio which all solution extensions by BlackLine customers can begin to utilize right away.   A key example is that BlackLine process templates and the status of BlackLine sub-processes can be viewed directly from within SAP Advanced Financial Closing which can further improve efficiency and create capacity across the financial close.

For more information on SAP Accounting Automation by BlackLine, please listen to the webinar or visit the following helpful links: 

short flyover video     long flyover video     video-blog     solution brief  SAP Help Portal

third party integration

Also see https://www.blackline.com/about/press-releases/ .

entry.title: Troubleshooting Ariba-Joule Integration errors
entry.content:
Introduction:🚀 Cracking the SAP Ariba–Joule Integration common errors:

Over the past few months, I’ve seen a common pattern across multiple customer implementations: brilliant landscapes struggling with Navigation Flow glitches, incomplete Transactional prompts, or inconsistent User Sync—all caused by a handful of tiny but critical configuration gaps across Ariba, IAS, IPS, and Build Work Zone.

To help accelerate troubleshooting and avoid post‑go‑live surprises, I’ve put together a practical breakdown of the most recurring issues and the exact fixes that have consistently stabilized the integration.

If you're enabling Joule for Ariba or supporting customers on their journey, this article will save you hours of guesswork. 🔧💡

Common issues with Navigation flow and resolution: 

Scenario 1:Navigational prompts intermittently return data

Sample prompts: Go my approvals, my task, create engagement request, created sourcing project, open supplier profile etc   

Cause: This could happen, if user has been assigned more than 150 groups in Ariba and those groups are being replicated to IAS and then to Build work zone.  To verify if the issue is due to roles replicated to BWZ,  login to BWZ and user will get error “HTTP ERROR 431”. 

Resolution: Replicate only those standard groups to BWZ which are required for Ariba-Joule integration. You can achieve this by adding a filter parameter ariba.applications.group.filter in the IPS source system (the source system configured to fetch users and groups from Ariba https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-integration-with-sap-ariba-setup-guide/ba-p/14240003 ). Using this filter ensures that only the required groups—and users assigned to those groups—are synchronized from Ariba to IAS and subsequently to BWZ.

Sample value: (displayName eq "Sourcing Agent" or displayName eq "Sourcing Approver")

Important Notes

  • Navigational flow works only with standard groups.
  • If customers assign only custom roles to users in Ariba, they must still replicate the required standard groups from Ariba → IAS → BWZ.
  • Custom groups are not supported, SAP is considering for future release 
  • This filtering process does not impact customer‑specific role assignments:
    • Customers may continue using their custom groups assignment in Ariba as usual
    • Since custom groups map back to standard roles, any Joule‑relevant standard roles tied to custom roles will still function properly through this filtered sync.
  • Recommendation:
    • Maintain a list of standard groups required for Joule navigation.
    • Additionally replicate customer‑specific custom groups only to ensure that all Ariba users sync correctly to CIS

Steps to maintain the parameter:

  • Login to CIS and navigate to identity provision
  • Source system à look for the source system which is created to fetch the user/groups from Ariba
  • Edit and maintain the parameter and run the IPS job as described previously
List of standard roles which are required for Ariba joule Navigational scenario:

Supplier Registration Manager

Supplier Manager

Supplier Qualification Manager

Supplier Risk ESG User

Purchasing Administrator

Supplier Risk Engagement Requestor

Financial Processing Analyst

Supplier Risk Evaluation Expert

AuditBuyer

Supplier Risk Provider Registration Expert

Purchasing Agent

Joule Supplier Profile User

Invoice Manager

Sourcing Project Administrator

Purchasing Manager

Sourcing Agent

Report Manager

Limited Event Administrator

Receiving Manager

Junior Sourcing Agent

Manual Order Processor

SM Questionnaire Manager

Tax Manager

Supplier Risk Engagement Governance Analyst

Purchasing User

Supplier Qualification Request Manager

Executive Approver

Supplier Risk User

Sourcing Approver

Supplier Risk Report Expert

Category Buyer

Supplier Risk Engagement Expert

Supplier Risk Engagement Analyst

Sourcing Manager

Operational Junior Sourcing Agent

Supplier Risk Manager

Supplier Risk Configuration Expert

Supplier Request Manager

SM Process Manager

Event Administrator

Scenario 2:  Error “The application cannot be opened”

Resolution: Create a destination under the sub-account which has been created for joule. Make sure destination name is “NavigationService” follow the below article for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/integrating-joule-with-sap/configure-destination-to-use-navigation-service?locale=en-US&q=+NavigationService

Steps:

  • Login to Joule Sub-account
  • Click connectivity à Destination
  • Click create

Scenario 3: Error: You need different permissions to perform this action or I am having trouble processing your request.

Cause : This happen when user doesn’t have the required access in Ariba or if they have the required access, joule related standard roles are not assigned to user in BWZ after replication.  

To verify the roles in BWZ, login to BWZ

  • Check User Role Assignments: In the settings à Identity Provisioning
  • Within "Explore Role Assignments",enter the user ID experiencing the issue
  • Review the list of roles and permissions assigned to the user

Resolution: As described in scenario 1 navigation flow, maintain the joule roles in source system parameter run the job. This will synchronize the user and groups between ariba , IAS and BWZ. Make sure roles are assigned to users in ariba as well. Login to BWZ and verify the roles once again:

Common issues with Transactional flow and resolution:

Scenario 1:When user enters any transactional prompt in joule, user gets results as an informational data.

Sample prompts: List of recent purchases

Cause: This happens when ibn_target not found at the backend. In simple words, the formation synchronization did not happen between the include system despite the fact status of the formation is ready.  

Resolution: Login to global account, exclude and include Ariba buyer and sourcing system within the formations.

 

Scenario 2: When user enters a transactional prompt, there is no response

Cause: This happens when there is no dependencies added to the das-ias applications in CIS

Resolution:

  • Login to IAS
  • Navigate to applications and search for “das-ias” and look for the entry which is there for the sub-account which is created for joule
  • Navigate to dependency section, make sure to maintain APIs for Procurement and Sourcing applications

Delayed User synchronization between Ariba and Cloud identify service: IPS Jobs are getting stuck in running status for few hours  

Steps to run the Job:

  • Login to CIS and navigate to identity provision

Source system à look for the source system which is created to fetch the user/groups from Ariba

 Click on job logs in IPS to check the status:

 Resolution:  By default the value configured for parameter (ariba.applications.group.members.page.size,ariba.applications.user.groups.page.size )  is 50, update it according to your need. If this doesn’t work, log a support ticket with SAP to increase the API rate limit.

Joule Icon Is Missing in Ariba despite completing all the configurations (running boosters, system added successfully in formations, SSO is enabled)

Resolution: Log a ticket with SAP to enable the feature PLUI 2928  at parent and child level

If you are facing any other issues, I would suggest to log a case with support team. To save time, make sure to share the logs and X-Correlation ID with support team. 

How to download the Joule Logs:

  • Login to Ariba application
  • Click on joule icon, reproduce the issue
  • Click on three bars --> settings  --> abouts --> download trances --> include message history --> download 

How to Get X Correlation ID?

  • Login to Ariba system
  • Enable logs:
    • Right-click on browser, select "Inspect"
    • once the Developer Tools are open, locate and click on the "Network" tab
  • Open Joule (keep the Developer Tools open)
  • Reproduce the issue you are experiencing
  • When the issue is reproduced:
    • in the Network tab, identify the most recent API call to Joule. This API call will be named with a UUID (for example: c1234b04-c313-42cd-9be3-a5efe9b0f2d1).
    • click on the API request and go to the "Headers" section;
    • scroll down to find and collect the following fields:
      • X-correlationid
      • Timestamp,
      • JouleDataCenter (you can find this in the "API Request URL")

Ariba-Joule support queues: you may choose the queue based on the nature of the error. 

✨Conclusion
The most common Ariba–Joule issues often stem from small but critical configuration gaps across Ariba, IAS, IPS, and Build Work Zone—and fixing these resolves the majority of navigation, transactional, and sync failures.
With the right configurations in place, Joule delivers the seamless, intelligent experience it’s designed for—helping customers unlock real value without post‑go‑live roadblocks. 🚀

entry.title: Legacy Excel to XML Conversion in Integration Suite: Handling BIFF5/Excel 5.0 during PI/PO migration
entry.content:
Migrating from PI/PO to Integration Suite comes with unexpected challenges - like handling legacy Excel files from the 90s. In this first post of our migration series, I'll show you how to convert those old BIFF5/Excel 5.0 files to XML when standard approaches fail. 

The Challenge

While excellent resources exist on Excel-to-XML conversion in CPI (see posts by @engswee, @yusufbayasal, and @RoohiJain), they assume modern Excel formats (BIFF8/XLSX). The Excel files we needed to convert were too old for these standard approaches, failing with the error:  "The supplied spreadsheet seems to be Excel 5.0/7.0 (BIFF5) format. POI only supports BIFF8 format (from Excel versions 97/2000/XP/2003)": 

javax.script.ScriptException: java.lang.Exception: 
org.apache.poi.hssf.OldExcelFormatException: 
The supplied spreadsheet seems to be Excel 5.0/7.0 (BIFF5) format. 
POI only supports BIFF8 format (from Excel versions 97/2000/XP/2003)

This error tells us something important: our Excel file is in the older BIFF5 format (used by Excel 5.0/7.0/95), which Apache POI's standard `HSSFWorkbook` and `XSSFWorkbook` classes cannot read. 

The solution

Apache POI provides a specialized utility class called OldExcelExtractor designed specifically for extracting text from these legacy Excel formats.

Preparation

Download poi jar file and imported into our CPI iFlow. To download it, go to the download page of Apache POI https://poi.apache.org/download.html >> Find the latest stable release >> select Binary Artifacts (https://repo1.maven.org/maven2/org/apache/poi/poi/5.5.1) >> select poi.x.x.x.jar. 

Upload it to the iFlow under References >> Add >> Archive, select the jar file and add. 

Implementation

We implmented the conversion in two steps: 

  • convert the Excel file into CSV format
  • convert CSV format into XML

Of couse one can combine both steps into the groovy script. But we did so to reduce custom scripting to minimum. 

Convert Excel to CSV

The script below has a hard-coded assumption about column count (every 10th row). Please adjust it to suite your needs. 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import org.apache.poi.hssf.extractor.OldExcelExtractor;

def Message processData(Message message) {
    byte[] bodyByteArray = message.getBody(java.io.InputStream).bytes
    OldExcelExtractor extractor = new OldExcelExtractor(new ByteArrayInputStream(bodyByteArray))
    String extractedText = extractor.getText()
    extractor.close()
    
    // Split into rows (OldExcelExtractor uses \n as row separator)
    def rows = extractedText.split('\n')
    def cvsData = ""
    
	//Convert tab-separated rows to pipe-separated format
	//Note: the legacy excel file has exactly 10 columns per row
    for(int i=1; i<rows.length; i++) {
        if (i % 10 == 0) {
            cvsData += rows[i] + '\n'
        } else {
            cvsData += rows[i] + '|'
        }
    }
    
    message.setBody(cvsData) 
    return message;
}

Convert CSV into XML

Here we used the XSD schema exported from PI/PO, and converting into XML skipping the first line. 

That's it. Old Excel formats shouldn't slow down your PI/PO migration. With Apache POI's `OldExcelExtractor` and the approach outlined above, you can automate the conversion of even the oldest Excel files - no manual intervention required.

Coming up next in this series: More real-world migration challenges and the exact solutions we used to solve them. Have you encountered legacy format issues in your migration? Drop a comment below!

entry.title: Re: Backofficesearchservices incompatible with adaptivese...
entry.content:
Dear rbespalovnc,

Hybris Commerce Cloud OOTB does not support the MULTISELECT facet type in ImpEx files when using Solr-based adaptive search.
While the ImpEx syntax accepts MULTISELECT, validation fails during import because SolrAsSearchProvider only supports MULTISELECT_OR, MULTISELECT_AND, and REFINE facet types.

To resolve import failures, replace all MULTISELECT references in ImpEx files with either MULTISELECT_OR (for OR logic) or MULTISELECT_AND (for AND logic) based on your facet behavior requirements

Kindly do validation in your lower environments.

Regards,

entry.title: SAP Job Scheduling Service REST API Now on Business Accelerator Hub - Explore and Try It Out!
entry.content:
🚀 SAP Job Scheduling Service REST API Now on Business Accelerator Hub - Explore and Try It Out!

Big news for SAP developers! The SAP Job Scheduling Service REST API is now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub (API Hub for short), making it easier than ever to discover, explore, and integrate job scheduling capabilities into your SAP BTP applications.

Whether you're building Cloud Foundry or Kyma applications, automating workflows, or managing background tasks, you can now find everything you need in one centralized location alongside other SAP APIs.

🎯 What's New?

As of February 18, 2026, the SAP Job Scheduling Service REST API is live on Business Accelerator Hub https://api.sap.com/

Why This Matters

Before this release, developers had to piece together API documentation from help portals, blog posts, and code examples. Now, everything is in one place with:

  • ✅ Centralized Discovery - Find the Job Scheduling Service API alongside 1000+ other SAP APIs
  • ✅ Interactive Testing - Try API endpoints directly in your browser with the "Try It Out" feature
  • ✅ OpenAPI 3.0.3 Specification - Standards-based documentation that works with any OpenAPI tool
  • ✅ Code Generation - Generate client libraries in your favorite programming language
  • ✅ Live Documentation - Always up-to-date API reference with request/response examples

🔍 What Can the Job Scheduling Service API Do?

The Job Scheduling Service REST API provides complete programmatic control over job scheduling on SAP BTP:

  • 📋 Job Management
  • 🕐 Schedule Management
  • 📊 Monitoring & Run Logs
  • 🔐 Authentication

🔎 How to Find the API on Business Accelerator Hub

Let's walk through discovering the Job Scheduling Service API on BAH:

Option 1: Search for "Job Scheduling service"

Search for "SAP Job Scheduling Service" directly in the search bar.

 

Option 2: Direct Link

Directly go to https://api.sap.com/api/sap-btpjss-admin-v1/overview

 

API Details Page

You'll see:

  • Overview - High-level description of the API capabilities
  • API Reference - Complete list of all endpoints organized by category
  • Schema View - Detailed definitions of request and response objects
  • Try It Out - Interactive testing of API endpoints with real authentication
  • Documents - Additional documentation links

📖 Exploring the API Documentation

The API documentation on BAH is organized into clear sections:

API Endpoints Overview

The Job Scheduling Service API groups endpoints into three main categories:

🔧 Jobs (/scheduler/jobs)

  • POST /jobs - Create a new job
  • GET /jobs - Retrieve all jobs
  • GET /jobs/{jobId} - Get details of a specific job
  • PATCH /jobs/{jobId} - Update an existing job
  • DELETE /jobs/{jobId} - Delete a job

📅 Schedules (/scheduler/jobs/{jobId}/schedules)

  • POST /jobs/{jobId}/schedules - Create a schedule for a job
  • GET /jobs/{jobId}/schedules - Get all schedules for a job
  • GET /jobs/{jobId}/schedules/{scheduleId} - Get a specific schedule
  • PATCH /jobs/{jobId}/schedules/{scheduleId} - Update a schedule
  • DELETE /jobs/{jobId}/schedules - Delete all schedules
  • DELETE /jobs/{jobId}/schedules/{scheduleId} - Delete a specific schedule

📊 Run Logs (/scheduler/jobs/{jobId}/schedules/{scheduleId}/runs)

  • GET /jobs/{jobId}/schedules/{scheduleId}/runs - Get execution logs
  • GET /jobs/{jobId}/schedules/{scheduleId}/runs/{runId} - Get details of a specific run

Schema Definitions

The API documentation includes detailed schema definitions for all request and response objects:

  • CreateJobRequest - Job creation with schedules
  • JobDetails - Complete job information
  • ScheduleDetails - Schedule configuration (cron, time, repeatInterval)
  • RunLog - Execution details, status, and HTTP responses
  • Error responses - Standard error format with messages and HTTP status codes

Each schema shows:

  • Property names and types
  • Required vs. optional fields
  • Default values
  • Validation constraints (min/max length, patterns, enums)
  • Example values

🎮 Try It Out - Test the API Interactively

One of the most powerful features of Business Accelerator Hub is the "Try It Out" functionality. Let's walk through testing a real API endpoint!

Prerequisites

Before you can test the API, you'll need:

  1. ✅ A Job Scheduling service instance on SAP BTP (Trial or Live)
  2. ✅ A service key with OAuth credentials

Don't have these yet? Check out our first blog post in the series for setup instructions.

Getting Your Service Key

  1. Open the SAP BTP Cockpit
  2. Navigate to your subaccount → space → Service Instances
  3. Find your Job Scheduling service instance
  4. Click "Create Service Key" (or use an existing one)
  5. Open the service key and note the following values:
    • url - The base URL for the REST API - extract the Landscape Region from this (e.g. eu10 from https://jobscheduler-rest.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com)
    • uaa.identityzone - The zone where the Identity Authentication service is located (e.g. my-subdomain.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com)
    • uaa.clientid - Your OAuth client ID
    • uaa.clientsecret - Your OAuth client secret

Example service key structure:

{
  "url": "https://jobscheduler-rest.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com",
  "uaa": {
    "identityzone": "my-subdomain",
    "clientid": "sb-clone-jobscheduler-service!b1234",
    "clientsecret": "your-secret-here"
  }
}
 

Step-by-Step: Testing GET /jobs

Let's test the GET /jobs endpoint to retrieve all jobs in your Job Scheduling service instance.

1. Configure Environment for Authentication

Click the "Select environment" button:

 

In the environment dialog enter:

  1. Display name - e.g. "My Trial instance"
  2. Landscape Region - e.g ap21 (trial) - extracted from the url
  3. Client ID - From your service key (uaa.clientid)
  4. Client Secret - From your service key (uaa.clientsecret)
  5. Cf-subaccount-domain - the domain of your BTP subaccount (e.g. dadb4adetrial) from identityzone
  6. Landscape Region - again - same as above (e.g. ap21) - yes, we need that, and yes it is confusing
 

If successful, you'll see a confirmation that the access token was obtained automatically.

2. Find the GET /jobs Endpoint

Navigate to the Jobs section and expand the GET /jobs endpoint and click "Run":

4. Configure Parameters (Optional)

The GET /jobs endpoint supports optional query parameters:

  • displaySchedules (boolean) - Include schedule details in response
  • page (integer) - Page number for pagination
  • pageSize (integer) - Number of jobs per page

For this example, we'll use the defaults (no parameters needed).

6. View the Response

You'll see the HTTP response directly in the browser:

Response Code: 200 OK

Response Body (example):

{
  "total": 2,
  "results": [
    {
      "jobId": 2523708,
      "name": "validateSalesOrder",
      "description": "Validates sales order requests",
      "action": "https://my-app.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/orders/validate",
      "active": true,
      "httpMethod": "POST",
      "jobType": "HTTP_ENDPOINT",
      "ansConfig": { "onError": true, "onSuccess": false },
      "calmConfig": { "enabled": false },
      "createdAt": "2026-02-15 10:30:00"
    },
    {
      "jobId": 2440430,
      "name": "dailyReport",
      "description": "Generate daily sales report",
      "action": "https://my-app.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/reports/daily",
      "active": true,
      "httpMethod": "GET",
      "jobType": "HTTP_ENDPOINT",
      "ansConfig": { "onError": false, "onSuccess": false },
      "calmConfig": { "enabled": true },
      "createdAt": "2026-02-10 08:00:00"
    }
  ],
  "prev_url": null,
  "next_url": null
}
 

🎉 Success! You've just called the Job Scheduling service REST API directly from your browser!

Understanding the Response

The response shows:

  • total - Total number of jobs in your instance
  • results - Array of job objects with:
    • jobId - Unique job identifier
    • name - Job name
    • description - Job description
    • action - HTTP endpoint that gets invoked
    • httpMethod - HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH)
    • active - Whether the job is currently active
    • jobType - Type of job (e.g. HTTP_ENDPOINT)
    • ansConfig - Alert Notification Service settings (onError, onSuccess)
    • calmConfig - SAP Cloud ALM integration settings (enabled)
    • createdAt - When the job was created (UTC timestamp)
  • prev_url / next_url - Pagination links for navigating result pages

Tips for Exploring Other Endpoints

Now that you know how "Try It Out" works, explore other endpoints:

Try creating a job:

  • Use POST /jobs with a sample request body
  • See the created job immediately in the response

Retrieve job details:

  • Copy a job ID from the GET /jobs response
  • Use GET /jobs/{jobId} to see full details including schedules

Check run logs:

  • Find a schedule ID from a job's schedules
  • Use GET /jobs/{jobId}/schedules/{scheduleId}/runs to see execution history

📚 Additional Resources

Official Documentation

  • SAP Job Scheduling Service Help Portal - Complete product documentation
  • Authentication Guide - OAuth 2.0 setup

Blog Series

  • Overview of All Blog Posts - Complete tutorial series

Discovery

  • SAP Discovery Center - Service overview and capabilities

🎯 What's Next?

Now that you know how to explore and test the Job Scheduling Service API on Business Accelerator Hub, you're ready for the next level:

📖 Coming Soon: Generate Your Own Job Scheduling Service Client Library

In our next blog post, we'll show you how to:

  • 🔧 Download the OpenAPI specification from Business Accelerator Hub
  • ⚙️ Generate a type-safe client library in your favorite language (Node.js, Java, Python, Go, etc.)
  • 💻 Use the generated client to create jobs, schedules, and monitor execution
  • 🚀 Build a complete working example with authentication and error handling

Whether you're building with Node.js, Java, Python, or another language, you'll be able to generate a custom client tailored to your needs!

Stay tuned! Subscribe to the SAP Job Scheduling Service blog series to get notified.

💬 Questions or Feedback?

Have questions about the Job Scheduling Service API on Business Accelerator Hub? Found a bug or have a feature request?

  • Idea - Submit your ideas on the Influence Portal
  • 📚 Help Portal - Check the official documentation and leave feedback
  • 💬 Community - Comment here in this post
  • 📧 Contact me - Find me on Linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/in/denis-duev/

🎉 Summary

The SAP Job Scheduling Service REST API is now discoverable on the Business Accelerator Hub, bringing:

  • ✅ One central location for API discovery alongside 1000+ SAP APIs
  • ✅ Interactive testing with "Try It Out" - no code required
  • ✅ OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification for standards-based integration
  • ✅ Up-to-date documentation with examples and schemas
  • ✅ Foundation for client generation (covered in our next blog!)

Whether you're just starting with Job Scheduling service or looking to integrate it into your SAP BTP applications, the Business Accelerator Hub makes it easier than ever to get started.

Happy Scheduling! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Successfactors - Custom MDF - Unique Identifier
entry.content:
Hi @RinyG ,

Unfortunately, I only came across this question now. Maybe my comments still help.

An onSave rule is called on every save regardless whether a new record is created or inserted, or an old record is updated. But you can prevent by a proper if condition that a field which is already filled is updated again. There are different possibilities:

  • Just check whether the field in question is "null". Then, the then action is only executed if the field had not been filled before. 
  • Check whether the "Original Record" is "null". This means that the rule is executed in a "Create" or "Insert", and not in a "Correct" or "Delete".
  • Check that the "Rule Context.Data Operation" is "Create" or "Insert".

The last two possibilities are only applicable to the MDF rule scenario but you are mentioning that you are in an MDF use case.

Please also note that if you only need a unique identifier and the sequence is irrelevant it is much preferable to use the rule function "Generate Unique Identifier()". Only if a sequence is required (and gaps don't matter), use the rule function "Get Next Value()". This rule function involves some costs because it always needs to update the Sequence object in a separate transaction during the rule function execution.

Kind regards, Heike

entry.title: Re: Intrastat Region
entry.content:
Good day @sanne_larsen 

Thank you for your question.

Just for clarification, in Intrastat dispatches, the system determines the Region from the delivering plant for example plant master address region, technical field like plant region. So, if the plant address has no region, or the region code is not maintained for that country, it will lead to the “Enter a valid region” error you received.

Error 1: Country of origin error.

Answer: Yes, your approach of maintaining Country of Origin in the material master is correct.

Error 2: Enter a valid region and where you should maintain it?

Answer: The system determines the Region from your delivering plant region. So, you should maintain Region in the delivering plant address, not manually in each transaction. If the region code is not valid in the system, maintain it using SSCUI 103826 Insert Regions. See KBA 3449400 on how to configure new regions.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Lock Parent vs Leaf Members
entry.content:
@Nikolaos ,

You could styling rules or Value lock Management. Data locks at Parent node depend on the lock state of its children. So if the leaf members ( holding data) are open they can be changed and as a result parent value will be changed. I would advise that you refer to the below SAP Note for an understanding of how the data locks work.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3041852

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Part 1: How to create CAPM project in BAS
entry.content:
Introduction: You could build a complete enterprise application - database, APIs, and UI - without different technologies? Well, answer is CAPM (Cloud Application Programming Model). CAPM  is a framework that provides a set of languages, libraries, and tools that allow you to build a complete full-stack application - covering the database, service layer, and UI.
It gives you everything you need in one coherent package: database modeling, service creation, and UI generation. No more context switching between completely different tools.

What we're building today:
  • A complete Employee-Department management system
  • From data model to working UI 

In this blog, we will create a simple full-stack CAP application and learn how to make basic connections between the data model, service, and UI.

We will cover the following topics:

  • Project creation

  • Data model creation

  • Service creation

  • UI creation

Step 1: Create a CAPM Project in BAS

To build a CAP project in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), you must use a Full Stack Cloud Application dev space.

Once your dev space is created and running, open it and proceed to Step 2.

Step 2: Open Terminal

Inside your BAS workspace:

  • Go to Terminal -> New Terminal
  • Run the following command to initialize a CAP project:

cds init demo1

This generates the project with the following structure:

  • db/ – Defines your entities (data model)

  • srv/ – Exposes your services

  • app/ – Your UI application.

Step 3: Create the Data Model

Create a file under db/ named schema.cds and add the following code:

namespace demo1;
using { cuid  } from '@sap/cds/common';


entity  Employees : cuid {
     name : String(50);
     adress : String(100);
     emailId: String(100);
     city: String(50);
     country : String(50);
     designation: String(50);
     salary: Decimal(10, 2);
     department: Association to Departments;
}

entity Departments : cuid {
     name : String(50);
     location : String(100);
     budget : Decimal(15, 2);
     employee: Association to many Employees
}

Step 4: Create the Service

Create a service.cds file under srv/ and expose your entities as a service:

 

using { demo1 as dataModel} from '../db/schema';

service EmployeeDepartmentService{
    entity Employees as projection on dataModel.Employees;
    entity Departments as projection on dataModel.Departments;
}

Now run the command in terminal

cds watch

Note: If you see "connection was rejected" Run the following:

npm uninstall better-sqlite3
npm install better-sqlite3

Then execute cds watch again.

Step 5: Test Using service.http

Create a file named service.http and add the following:

# GET http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/employee-department/Employees

POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/employee-department/Employees
Content-Type: application/json

{
    "name": "Nila", 
    "adress": "Chennai", 
    "emailId": "nila@gmail.com", 
    "city": "Anytown", 
    "country": "USA", 
    "designation": "Software Engineer", 
    "salary": "40000.00"
}

Click Send Request and you should receive 200 OK if everything works correctly.

Step 6: Create the UI5 Application

  1. Go to File → Create Project from Template
  2. Select List Report Object Page (or Freestyle UI5 — here we choose List Report)
  3. Click Next

Data Source & Service Selection

  • Data Source: Use a local CAP project

  • Choose Project: Select demo1

  • OData Service: It will automatically pick EmployeeDepartmentService

Click Next

Entity Selection

  • Main Entity: Employees
  • Table Type: Grid
    Click Next

Project Name

  • Enter: demo1ui
  • Click Finish

Your UI5 application is now generated.

step 7: View the UI

  • You can run the UI by opening index.html inside the web application below.
  • You can also check the metadata and OData results directly.

Step 8: Use “Show Page Map”

  • Right-click webapp → Show Page Map.

This provides a visual overview of the app structure.
From here, you can create:

  • Table columns
  • Sections
  • Actions
  • Object Page content, etc..

Below I added the required table columns.

Step 9: Object Page Navigation

Once the user clicks an entry from the List Report, the app navigates to the Object Page and displays the configured fields.

Step 10: Test Data in UI

This is the initial screen After sending the POST request from service.http, refresh your UI5 app.

 The inserted records will appear in the table by clicking Go.

Note: SQLite is used in-memory for local CAP testing.
This means data is cleared every time the server stops, this is for development mode.

Conclusion:  This blog is just the starting point for learning CAPM. Understanding these basic concepts is very important. You now understand how CAP connects database, services, and UI seamlessly. This foundation will help you build real business application

entry.title: How to Pass a Filter Parameter to OData V4 TreeTable Hierarchy Batch Calls in Fiori Elements?
entry.content:
I am building a Fiori Elements List Report page with a TreeTable using the sap.fe.templates.ListReport template and OData V4. The table uses SAP Hierarchy annotations (com.sap.vocabularies.Hierarchy.v1) with a hierarchyQualifier.

My manifest configuration looks like this:

"controlConfiguration": {
    "@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem": {
        "tableSettings": {
            "type": "TreeTable",
            "hierarchyQualifier": "ResponseHierarchy",
            "selectionMode": "None"
        }
    }
}

The Problem

When the page loads, Fiori Elements automatically fires 3 batch calls:

GET ResponseHierarchy(db05dc96-ea30-4393-a929-266e1c8d2331)
GET ResponseHierarchy/$count
GET ResponseHierarchy?$apply=com.sap.vocabularies.Hierarchy.v1.TopLevels(
    HierarchyNodes=$root/ResponseHierarchy,
    HierarchyQualifier='ResponseHierarchy',
    NodeProperty='ID',
    Levels=1
)&$select=...&$count=true&$skip=0&$top=240

I need to pass a programCycleId to all 3 calls so my backend handler knows which program's data to fetch. The first call works fine because the ID is part of the key segment. However, calls 2 and 3 hit the entity set directly ($root/ResponseHierarchy) without any key or filter.

What I Have Tried

1. Passing ID as route key

{ "pattern": "ResponseHierarchy({ID}):?query:" }

This works for call 1 but calls 2 and 3 don't carry the ID.

2. Passing $filter as query parameter

oParams = { query: { "$filter": "programCycleId eq '" + sProgramId + "'" } };

The $filter in the route URL is not propagated to the $apply=TopLevels(...) and $count calls. The TreeTable control generates these calls referencing $root/ResponseHierarchy directly, completely ignoring the URL $filter.

3. Custom HTTP Header

I ended up using a workaround, setting a custom header x-program-cycle-id on the OData model and reading it in the backend:

oModel.changeHttpHeaders({ "x-program-cycle-id": sProgramCycleId });

This works but it's not ideal because:

  • The header is set globally on the model, affecting all other OData calls
  • It requires manual cache busting (getAllBindings().filter(...).forEach(b => b.refresh()))
  • It's not a clean OData-native solution

My Question

Is there a proper OData V4 / Fiori Elements native way to pass a filter or parameter that gets included in ALL 3 TreeTable hierarchy batch calls, including the $apply=TopLevels(HierarchyNodes=$root/ResponseHierarchy...) call?

Specifically I would like to know:

  1. Is it possible to configure the TreeTable binding via annotations so that a $filter is always appended to all hierarchy calls including the $root/ResponseHierarchy reference inside $apply?

  2. Is there a manifest.json setting or UI5 Fiori Elements API that allows scoping the hierarchy data to a specific filter value without resorting to custom headers?

  3. Is the custom header approach actually the recommended workaround for this scenario, or is there a better pattern?

Any guidance or pointers to documentation would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Timesheet Time Entry with Multiple Activity Allocatio...
entry.content:
Hello @S0025758586 

Thank you for your question and the description you provided. Below are answers.

Question: is there such functionality of multiple activity allocation from single timesheet entry supported in standard?

Answer: No, the Public Cloud, one timesheet entry is designed for one activity type posting which is activity-based time recording and does not change automatically into multiple activity allocations.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/203be656b9352f7be10000000a44147b.html?locale=en-US

Question 2: If not, is there a tool to derivate multiple activity allocations with reference to source entry ? 

Answer: Not in the Public Cloud. The standard alternative is either:

  • You enter multiple timesheet lines (T001, T002, T003) that ensures all lines references the same WBS and you make use of copy to reduce your effort (to reduce your user efforts, i recommend you standardise using templates), or
  • If your intention is “indirect cost approximation”, rather than actual secondary activity quantity postings, then you should use standard Overhead Calculation on WBS instead of trying to post multiple types from one record.

Question 3:  is there another procedure under this scope to allocate overheads from cost centers to WBS elements?

Answer: Yes, the available procedural options are:

  • Overhead Calculation on WBS. You can use the overhead calculation apps and configuration.

The following reference guides you on how to run it:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/b25561e3dd3c44ec846b77d35737bc24.html?locale=en-US

  • Universal Allocation to allocate cost center actuals periodically to WBS by making use of allocation cycles.

See the following KBA for more details.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2860376

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Custom Consignment Process
entry.content:
Following is the requirement that I can see from your question.

You perform Fill up to Customer XX with 130 qty. & customer XX further supplies the product to customers as mentioned below.

1. Customer A - M10 - 100 Qty

2. Customer B - M10 - 10 Qty

3. Customer C - M10 - 20 Qty.

Once these qtys. are sold, you bill to Customer A, B & C. Hope my understanding is right.

In such case, you can play around with the partner functions as below.

1. Consignment Fill-up order - Partner functions SP, SH, BP, PY = Customer XX

2. Consignment Issue order - Partner functions SP & SH = Customer XX || Partner functions BP & PY = Customer A or Customer B or Customer C, depending on who you want to bill.

Thanks, 

Prashant

entry.title: Customized Logo and Splash Screen in MDK Mobile App for Android
entry.content:
This blog explains how to customize the app launcher icon (logo) and the splash screen for an Android customized client built using SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK).

The approach described here is based on real project experience with an MDK customized client built using tools provided by SAP and targeting the Android platform maintained by Google.

When do we need Android branding in MDK?

You normally customize the Android logo and splash screen when:

  • you build a customized MDK client

  • your customer requires corporate branding

  • the app will be distributed internally or through an app store

This is different from a standard MDK client where you cannot change native branding.

 

Android branding folder in MDK customized app

All Android-specific branding is handled inside:

\project_folder\App_Resources_Merge\Android\src\main\res

You should only work inside the native resource folders and only replace images.

Customizing the Android App Logo (Launcher Icon) :

In Android, the launcher icon is maintained using the standard Android mipmap folders.

In an MDK customized app, go to:

App_Resources/Android/src/main/res/

You will find folders such as (if not create the folders):

  • mipmap-mdpi

  • mipmap-hdpi

  • mipmap-xhdpi

  • mipmap-xxhdpi

  • mipmap-xxxhdpi

Inside each folder, there will already be an existing launcher icon file ( if not generate the icon file from android studio asset).

What you should do

  • Replace the image file with your customer logo

  • Keep:

    • the same file name

    • the same folder structure

What you should not do

  • Do not rename the icon files

  • Do not delete any mipmap folders

  • Do not change any Android manifest or build files

MDK and the Android project already reference these resource names internally.

It will look like this after adding the icon: 

Customizing the Android Splash Screen

The splash screen is the first screen shown when the MDK client starts.

In an Android MDK customized app, the splash screen image is usually stored under:

App_Resources_Merge/Android/src/main/res/drawable/

You will see a default splash image used by the MDK client.

How to customize

  • Replace the existing splash image with your branded image

  • Keep the same file name

If your project uses a background color together with the splash image, the color is usually defined in the Android style configuration (for example in ic_launcher_background.xml).
In most MDK projects, you do not need to modify this unless your branding requires a different background color.

After adding drawable folder and logo , following structure will be like this : 

Important rule for Android MDK branding

In an MDK customized Android app:

Only replace image resources.

Do not modify:

  • native Java / Kotlin files

  • AndroidManifest.xml

  • Gradle configuration

  • MDK internal resources

Changing anything beyond images can cause build failures or runtime issues during future MDK or Android upgrades.

 

Conclusion

In an Android customized MDK client, changing the app logo and splash screen is a simple and safe activity if done correctly.

The key point is:

For Android MDK branding, only replace the existing image resources under
App_Resources/Android/src/main/res/ and never change native configuration or code files.

Following this approach ensures that your branded MDK Android app remains stable and upgrade-safe.

entry.title: Re: Parent–Child Material Sales Scenario for MTS (Make-to...
entry.content:
Possible Approaches in Public Cloud

1) Sales BOM + Material Determination
Use the parent material as the order entry point. Configure material determination rules so that if one child is not available, another child can be substituted. This requires predefined substitution logic (e.g., child A → child B → child C).

2) Advanced ATP (aATP)
In S/4HANA Cloud, aATP offers product substitution and alternative materials functionality. You can define Product Substitution Lists so that if the requested material is not available, the system proposes alternatives. This is the closest fit to your requirement, but it still requires configuration of substitution rules — it won’t dynamically pick “any child with stock” unless you define the substitution sequence.

Recommendation

  • Activate scope item 4A6 (Advanced ATP) in your Public Cloud tenant.
  • Define Product Substitution Lists for your parent material, mapping to child materials.
  • Configure substitution priority so the system checks stock sequentially.
  • Train users to expect that the sales order will show the substituted child material, not the parent.

entry.title: Re: Purchasing currency USD invoice paid in EUR base curr...
entry.content:
How SAP handles this

1) Invoice Posting (USD → INR)
When you post the vendor invoice, SAP records the amount in USD and translates it into INR using the exchange rate valid on the invoice date. This establishes the liability in both USD and INR.

2) Payment Posting (EUR → INR)
When you pay in EUR, SAP records the outgoing payment in EUR and translates it into INR using the exchange rate valid on the payment date. The system then clears the USD liability against the EUR payment.

3) Currency Reconciliation
Because the invoice was in USD and the payment was in EUR, SAP automatically calculates the exchange rate difference in INR. This difference is posted to the Exchange Rate Difference account (configured in OB09 for FI). The reconciliation ensures that the vendor account is cleared, and any FX gain/loss is recognized in INR.

entry.title: Scaling Generative AI in SAP S/4HANA: Enterprise-Ready Innovation with ISLM
entry.content:
• Introduction and context
  • Key Enhancements for Generative AI Enablement in ISLM
  • Availability and Downport Strategy Across SAP S/4HANA Releases
  • Deep Dive: Latest ISLM Capabilities for Generative AI
  • Orchestration Service Integration
  • Developing Custom Generative AI Use Cases — Step-by-Step guide
  • Support for new models offered by Generative AI Hub
  • Developing Your Own AI-Enabled Applications in SAP ABAP Cloud with ISLM and ABAP AI SDK
  • Conclusion

Introduction and context

In today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape, Generative AI  is transforming how enterprises innovate, automate, and deliver business value . To successfully embed Generative AI within SAP S/4HANA, organizations need more than access to large language models. They require a structured, scalable, and governed framework to integrate, operate, and manage AI use cases across their landscape.

Over the past year, these Generative AI capabilities are now enabled through ISLM, helping customers and partners move from experimentation to productive usage. You can learn more through my previous blog post and the  YouTube webinar replay which outline the architecture, supported use cases, and how ISLM integrates Generative AI into SAP S/4HANA SAP business applications. 

Building on this foundation, this blog focuses on the latest and upcoming enhancements in Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management that further strengthen Generative AI enablement. We will explore what’s new, how these enhancements simplify GenAI integration and lifecycle management, and how customers and partners can leverage them to accelerate innovation and scale AI-driven business scenarios in SAP S/4HANA.

Key Enhancements for Generative AI Enablement in ISLM

  1. Integration with the Orchestration Service:  ISLM now integrates with the Orchestration Service to deliver advanced, enterprise-grade  capabilities for Generative AI scenarios. This includes Document Grounding to enrich prompts with business-relevant context, Content Filtering to ensure safe and compliant responses, and Data Masking to protect sensitive information before it is processed by large language models. In addition, the service supports input and output translation for multilingual use cases and incorporates a Prompt Shield filter to help defend against prompt injection attacks. Together, these capabilities significantly enhance the security, reliability, and governance of Generative AI embedded within SAP business applications.
  2. Support for new models offered by Generative AI Hub: ISLM enables customers to leverage state-of-the-art large language models while maintaining flexibility and future readiness.
  3. Reuse Connectivity to Gen AI Hub:  A shared connectivity model allows a single connection between the ABAP system and the SAP Generative AI Hub to be configured once and reused across multiple AI use cases. This simplifies administration, improves governance, and reduces setup effort.
  4. Simplification of creation of Intelligent Scenario: Creation of Intelligent Scenarios has been simplified, moving from a pro-code approach to a more streamlined low-code experience.In addition, ISLM allows the optional use of the prediction class for Generative AI scenarios. 

Availability and Downport Strategy Across SAP S/4HANA Releases 

To accelerate the adoption of Generative AI, the latest ISLM enhancements have been downported to lower SAP S/4HANA releases, enabling customers to benefit from new capabilities without waiting for a full system upgrade. These enhancements are available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

Supported Releases (No TCI Required)

For the following Support Package (SP) levels, the latest capabilities are already included — no additional TCI implementation is required:

  • SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01
  • SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS06

Lower SP Levels (TCI Required)

For lower SP levels within the 2025 and 2023 releases, the relevant Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCIs) must be implemented to enable the latest ISLM capabilities.

By delivering these enhancements via TCIs, customers can adopt the newest ISLM innovations without requiring a complete Support Package update. This approach significantly reduces upgrade complexity while providing faster access to the latest Generative AI functionality in ISLM.

To enable the new capabilities, please implement the corresponding SAP Notes listed below.

  • SAP Note 3682148
  • SAP Note 3668194

Deep Dive: Latest ISLM Capabilities for Generative AI

Orchestration Service Integration

ISLM supports integration with the Orchestration Service on SAP AI Core, enabling advanced Generative AI capabilities such as document grounding, content filtering, and data masking. Additional features like input and output translation and a Prompt Shield filter help protect against prompt injection attacks, strengthening security and trust. New API enhancements further improve grounding quality through larger chunk sizes and application-specific document chunking. Together, these capabilities enable standardized, flexible, and enterprise-ready GenAI integration—allowing customers to switch between foundation models without code changes and reinforcing ISLM as the central framework for governing and scaling Generative AI across SAP landscapes.

The following modules within the orchestration service are supported by ISLM :

  1. Data Masking – Protects sensitive information by masking or removing confidential data.
  2. Input Filtering – Validates and sanitizes incoming data before it is processed by the LLM.
  3. Output Filtering – Reviews and filters the LLM-generated output to ensure compliance and relevance.
  4. Grounding – Aligns model outputs with enterprise data or domain-specific knowledge to improve response accuracy.
Developing Custom Generative AI Use Cases — Step-by-Step guide

Following information is relevant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition & On-Premise releases 

For step-by-step guide on using orchestration within ISLM, let’s consider the following example use case:

Use Case: AI-Driven Return Policy Analysis with ISLM

In this scenario, two fictional sports brands — Company Alpha and Company Beta — maintain different return policies with varying eligibility rules, timelines, refund conditions, and exceptions.

Customer support teams frequently handle queries such as:

  • “Is this item still eligible for return?”

  • “Are customized products refundable?”

  • “How long will the refund take?”

Manually reviewing policy documents is time-consuming and can lead to inconsistent responses.

Using ISLM with orchestration:

  • Policy documents are uploaded for document grounding.

  • User queries are processed through the orchestration workflow.

  • The LLM extracts relevant clauses and generates structured responses.

Business Value

  • Reduces manual effort

  • Speeds up response time

  • Ensures consistent policy interpretation

  • Enables automated decision support within business processes

Implementation Steps 

  1. Set and configure SAP AI Core
  2. Design and development of the Orchestration Workflow
  3. Export workflow configuration
  4. Create and deploy Intelligent Scenarios 
  5. Consumption of the Intelligent Scenario with ABAP AI SDK APIs

Prerequisites for using Gen AI scenario in ISLM. Ensure that you have completed the following steps before continuing with creating your own Gen AI scenario: Prerequisites | SAP Help Portal

1) Access AI Launchpad: Design and Development of the Orchestration Workflow
Open an available instance of the SAP Generative AI Hub – >AI Launchpad to start prompt engineering and configure your orchestration workflow.

1.1) Configure Orchestration Modules
Within the AI Launchpad, configure the required modules for the orchestration service according to your use case, refer to the following link:  Build Your Orchestration Workflow | SAP Help Portal

Support for new models offered by Generative AI Hub

With the ABAP AI SDK, powered by ISLM, support has been extended to large language models (LLMs), including the following:

gpt-5
gpt-5-mini
gpt-5-nano
o3
o4-mini
gemini-2.5-flash
gemini-2.5-pro
amazon--nova-pro
amazon--nova-lite
amazon--nova-micro
mistralai--mistral-medium-instruct
mistralai--mistral-small-instruct

This enhancement broadens model support, enabling alignment with varied enterprise requirements.

For detailed information on supported models, availability, please refer to the relevant SAP Note 3668194 .

Developing Your Own AI-Enabled Applications in SAP ABAP Cloud with ISLM and ABAP AI SDK 

By leveraging ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM), you can enrich your custom ABAP applications with seamlessly integrated Generative AI capabilities. This enables you to embed intelligent, context-aware features directly into your business processes while maintaining governance and clean core principles. This approach enables customers to extend SAP applications in a future-proof and upgrade-safe manner. 

Together, ISLM and ABAP AI SDK offer a powerful yet governed framework for embedding Generative AI into ABAP applications, ensuring enterprise-level reliability, security, and scalability. 

For detailed  guidance and  steps, refer to the SAP Help Portal | Developing Your Own AI-Enabled Applications: 

To get started, refer to the SAP Help Portal  .

Conclusion

Generative AI delivers real enterprise value when it is embedded directly into business processes with the right governance, scalability, and control. Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management  provides exactly that foundation, enabling structured creation, orchestration, deployment, and lifecycle management of AI scenarios within SAP S/4HANA.

With advanced runtime capabilities such as Document Grounding, Content Filtering, Data Masking, multilingual support,  reusable connectivity, and expanded model support, ISLM transforms Generative AI from experimentation into operational reality. Get started today and take your Generative AI innovation to the next level with ISLM!!

entry.title: Assistance Required for SAP HANA System Replication (HSR) & Scale‑Up High Availability Configuration
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have completed the installation of the SAP HANA database on both the primary node (currently running with business data) and the secondary node (with no business data).

I am seeking your assistance in configuring SAP PERSON_NAME System Replication (HSR) and setting up a Scale‑Up High Availability (HA) environment for our SAP HANA database.

At present, neither the system replication nor the HA setup has been configured. We request your guidance and support to complete the configuration according to SAP best practices.

We request SAP’s support for the following activities:

  1. Guidance on configuring SAP HANA System Replication (HSR) between the primary and secondary nodes.
  2. Assistance with the correct configuration sequence, including:
    • Initial data load
    • Secondary node registration
    • Activation of replication
    • Validation and health checks
  3. Recommendations for Scale‑Up High Availability setup, including best practices for:
    • Replication mode (sync / syncmem / async)
    • Operation mode (logreplay)
    • Cluster integration with Pacemaker (if applicable)
  4. Verification that the planned architecture is aligned with SAP recommendations and supported configurations.
  5. Clarification on any prerequisite checks, logs, or additional information required from our side to proceed.

We are ready to provide diagnostic logs, system replication outputs, landscape information, or any other details upon request.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required for SAP HANA System Replication (...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Please take a look at the answers below for your questions.

1.Guidance on configuring SAP HANA System Replication (HSR) between the primary and secondary nodes.
A: Please refer to 'General Prerequisites for Configuring SAP HANA System Replication' in System Replication guide. 

2.Assistance with the correct configuration sequence, including:
A: You can set up the System Replication in various ways. 
   Please refer to 'Configuring SAP HANA System Replication' in System Replication guide. 
   
   '3. Which indications exist for problems with the SAP HANA system replication?' KBA 1999880 - FAQ:SAP HANA System Replication.
   
3.Recommendations for Scale Up High Availability setup, including best practices for:
A: Please refer to the following documents and KBA.
   Replication Modes for SAP HANA System Replication 
   Operation Modes for SAP HANA System Replication 
   '35. What is the logreplay operation mode?' in KBA 1999880 - FAQ:SAP HANA System Replication. 
   

4.Verification that the planned architecture is aligned with SAP recommendations and supported configurations.
A: The current OS version SLES 15 SP3 is supported with HANA 2.0 SPS07.
   For details, please refer to KBA 2235581 - SAP HANA: Supported Operating Systems. 

5.Clarification on any prerequisite checks, logs, or additional information required from our side to proceed.
A: Please refer to the answer 1 above.
   You can see the details about the prerequisites for configuring System Replication there.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: #CoE CSRI is there any tool to support migrating ECC...
entry.content:
Hi Eric,

There’s no automatic tool that directly migrates SAP ECC custom code to the public cloud. Instead, the project team should review each piece of custom code to understand its purpose and decide the best extensibility approach.

Please find more from blog blow,

https://community.sap.com/t5/cloud-erp-public-value-map-knowledge-base/faq-assessing-custom-code-migration-from-sap-ecc-to-sap-s-4hana-public/ta-p/14238934

entry.title: Installation of Crystal Reports 2025 damages installation of Crystal Reports for Visual Studio SP 39
entry.content:
We have this behavior on several development PCs.

What was installed:

  • Crystal Report 2020 SP05
  • Crystal Reports for Visual Studio SP 39 (32 bit)

Before installing CR 2025 we uninstalled CR 2020 (and its SPs).
After installing CR 2025 (RTM Patch 800) we started our ERP program.

When trying to print we got the following exception:
Message: TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for 'CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument' threw an exception.
Source: CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine

Message: FileNotFoundException: Retrieving the COM class factory for component with CLSID {4DB2E2BB-78E6-4AEA-BEFB-FDAAB610FD1B} failed due to the following error: 8007007e The specified module could not be found. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x8007007E).
Source: CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine

Workaround: when we install "Crystal Reports for Visual Studio SP 39" again, the exception no longer comes.

So there should be a compatibility problem between CR 2025 and CRVS SP39?

Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: How to check the change log of sales prices 'Manage Price-Sales' - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
How to check the change log of sales prices/conditions in 'Manage Price-Sales' .


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check the change log of sales prices 'Manage P...
entry.content:
It is not able to check the change log in Manage Prices - Sales app at present.

An alternative approach that may help you achieve your goal:  

  1. Open "Set Material- Prices" app.
  2. Fill out Condition Type and click Key Combination, then choose.
  3. Fill out all the required entry fields, then click Execute.
  4. Select the condition records.
  5. In the upper menu click More-> Environment -> Changes -> Per condition record/Change record, then it will be able to see the change log of each record.

Refer KBA 3135462 - How to check the change log of sales prices - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

entry.title: Introducing prebuilt Joule skills in SAP Build digital workspaces: Get more done with simple prompts
entry.content:
If your day involves spinning up new spaces, hunting for the right workspace, or sharing content with your team, you know the clicks can add up. SAP Build simplifies this in its digital workspace experience with new prebuilt Joule skills which turns plain‑language prompts into actions. Ask Joule to create or find a workspace and it does the work in the background- then gives you a direct link in the chat so you can jump right in

The first set of prebuilt Joule skills are already live. Take a look at what’s available today: 

 

What’s in this first set of skills

  • Create workspaces from a prompt
    • Example: “Create a workspace called ‘Q3 Onboarding Hub’ using the standard onboarding template.”
    • Joule creates and names the workspace behind the scenes and returns a link in the chat.
  • Find workspaces you need
    • Example: “Show me all workspaces I’m a member of related to ‘Onboarding’.”
    • Joule lists relevant workspaces right in the chat, helping you get where you need to go faster.
  • Add documents right from chat
    • Drag and drop a file into the Joule chat, confirm the target workspace, and Joule adds it for you- no tab hopping required.
  • Find and add members to workspaces
    • Tell Joule who you want to add to a workspace, and Joule finds the person, confirms and sends them an invitation to join the workspace.

Why this matters

For teams needing a space to collaborate and produce results, these skills simplify their day-to-day so that -

  • members spend less time on common setup tasks like launching a project hub, onboarding center, or campaign space,
  • users can move from “search and navigate” to “ask and act” without leaving their flow of work,
  • workspace creators get consistency in content through standardized workspace templates to get started quickly, 
  • business users get more self-service options and admins get fewer “how do I create/find X?” tickets.

Prebuilt skills can be used in conjunction with the existing AI capabilities already available in digital workspaces. For example, once you find or create workspaces via Joule, you can use embedded AI assistance to create and publish content or summarize documents within the workspace.

Further, Joule can be enabled with document grounding for workspaces so that it answers questions based on content maintained in workspaces and links back to sources. This way its responses are grounded and relevant to business context.

Admins and developers can also build custom skills for Joule using Joule Studio and use them in digital workspaces.

Everyday scenarios where you can use these skills:

  • HR onboarding hub: “Create a workspace called ‘New Hire Hub – July’ based on the onboarding template.” Share the link with HR/ team managers in minutes.
  • Project kickoff: “Find all workspaces mentioning ‘Apollo Launch’.” Jump straight into the right workspace for plans, assets, and status.
  • Policy refresh: “Create a workspace named ‘FY26 Policy Updates’.” Drag and drop updated PDFs into the chat to populate the workspace content folder quickly.
  • Campaign workspace: “Create ‘Q2 Partner Enablement’.” Then use AI writing assistance on the landing page to publish a clean, on‑brand overview.
  • Knowledge triage: “Show my ‘Customer Escalations’ workspaces.” Get a list in chat and dive into the relevant one to update content or add the right members to solve the issue.

Getting started with Joule’s prebuilt skills and embedded AI in digital workspaces:

    • First, the admin must ensure Work Zone is enabled as a Joule launcher as per the instructions in this guide
    • Once set up is done, open Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and try prompts like
      • “Create a workspace named ‘Team All‑Hands’.”
      • “Find my workspaces related to ‘Compliance’.”
    • Ask your admin to enable document grounding for the workspaces you rely on- so Joule can answer questions with citations.
    • Ask your admin to enable embedded AI features to use AI assistance when editing pages or posts to speed up content creation once your workspace is ready or to summarize your documents quickly. See how you can use embedded AI for document summarization or workspace content creation here: 

 

Tips for Best Results for interacting with Joule:

  • Be specific with search keywords while searching for workspaces or content with Joule
  • While prompting give sufficient context to help Joule understand the task.

What’s next
This is just the first set of prebuilt skills we’re releasing for digital workspaces. We’re continuing to expand the catalog with more actions and smarter multi-step flows.
